skill.name: temporal-python-testing
skill.description: Guides Temporal Python testing with pytest, time-skipping workflow environments, mocked activities, replay testing, and local development setup.
skill.summary: Build reliable pytest coverage for Temporal Python workflows, activities, and replay tests.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown code fences, documentation examples, and the word skill being misread as weak crypto. The main confirmed concern is legitimate replay-testing guidance that exports production workflow histories to local files, which can expose sensitive workflow data if users do not protect or sanitize it.

medium_findings.title: Production History Export Requires Data Handling Controls
medium_findings.description: The replay-testing guide shows connections to a production Temporal endpoint and writes workflow histories to local .pb files. This is a legitimate Temporal replay-testing pattern, but workflow histories can contain business data and should be sanitized, access-controlled, and excluded from source control.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples explicitly fetch production workflow histories and write serialized histories to disk. The pattern is legitimate for replay testing, so the concern is data exposure rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external-command findings are false positives caused by fenced Markdown examples such as Python, YAML, Bash, and Makefile blocks. The skill is documentation and does not include executable scripts that run these examples automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The alerts align with Markdown fence boundaries and example snippets. No file contains code that automatically invokes a shell or dynamically executes user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Substring False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts point to the skill name and skill description, not cryptographic logic. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic operations in the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata and resource guidance using the word skill. They do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashes, keys, or encryption instructions.

low_findings.title: Network URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are localhost, documentation comments, or an explicit anti-pattern showing why workflow code should not call external APIs directly. These examples do not exfiltrate data from the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The local setup URLs point to local services, and the API example is labeled non-deterministic. The production endpoint examples are handled separately as a data-governance concern.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access Is Replay Fixture Handling
low_findings.description: The file read, glob, and hidden-path findings are examples for loading workflow histories and linking to a GitHub workflow directory. They are not hidden persistence, credential harvesting, or unauthorized file traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem examples use fixed workflow history paths for replay tests. The hidden-file alert is a .github path in an additional-resource URL, not local hidden file access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Production History Export Requires Data Handling Controls
dangerous_patterns.description: The replay-testing guide shows connections to a production Temporal endpoint and writes workflow histories to local .pb files. This is a legitimate Temporal replay-testing pattern, but workflow histories can contain business data and should be sanitized, access-controlled, and excluded from source control.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples explicitly fetch production workflow histories and write serialized histories to disk. The pattern is legitimate for replay testing, so the concern is data exposure rather than malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Temporal workflow tests need deterministic patterns, reliable fixtures, and clear replay checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for pytest-based Temporal Python testing.
actual_capabilities: Explains pytest fixtures for Temporal WorkflowEnvironment and ActivityEnvironment.
Shows time-skipping patterns for long-running workflow tests.
Provides mocked activity examples for integration tests.
Guides replay testing against stored workflow histories.
Outlines local Docker Compose setup for Temporal development.
Includes coverage, CI, and test organization guidance.
limitations: It provides documentation and examples, not a runnable test framework.
Examples must be adapted to each project workflow and activity names.
Replay history exports may require data protection and retention controls.
Temporal SDK APIs may need version checks before direct use.

use_cases.title: Add Workflow Unit Tests
use_cases.description: Create fast pytest coverage for workflow branches, sleeps, retries, and activity behavior.

use_cases.title: Validate Deployment Safety
use_cases.description: Use replay tests to check that workflow changes remain deterministic before production release.

use_cases.title: Standardize Temporal Test Setup
use_cases.description: Document local server setup, pytest configuration, coverage targets, and CI jobs for a team.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Workflow Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me write a pytest test for one Temporal Python workflow with time-skipping.

prompt_templates.title: Mock Activities in Integration Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design Temporal Python integration tests that mock external activity dependencies and verify retry behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Build Replay Test Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan replay tests for existing Temporal workflow histories before I deploy workflow code changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Temporal Test Suite
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across unit, integration, and replay tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Temporal Python test strategy for coverage gaps, nondeterminism risks, and CI readiness.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to test a workflow that waits 30 days before sending a reminder.
output_examples.output: A focused test plan using time-skipping, a reusable workflow environment fixture, and assertions for intermediate and final states.

output_examples.input: A team wants to verify that payment workflow refactoring will not break running executions.
output_examples.output: A replay testing checklist that covers representative histories, failure handling, CI integration, and protected storage for exported histories.

output_examples.input: A project has slow Temporal tests and inconsistent coverage reporting.
output_examples.output: A local setup and pytest organization plan with markers, coverage thresholds, and separate unit, integration, and replay test paths.

best_practices: Keep workflow logic deterministic and move external calls into activities.
Use time-skipping and mocked activities for fast, isolated feedback.
Protect production workflow histories and exclude exported files from source control.
anti_patterns: Do not call real third-party APIs directly from workflow code during tests.
Do not commit exported production workflow histories to a repository.
Do not rely only on end-to-end tests for workflow behavior coverage.

faq.question: Does this skill run Temporal tests for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides testing patterns and guidance that you adapt to your codebase.

faq.question: What Temporal test types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers unit tests, integration tests with mocked activities, replay tests, and local setup.

faq.question: Can it help with long-running workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains time-skipping so workflows with long sleeps can be tested quickly.

faq.question: Is replay testing safe with production histories?
faq.answer: It can be safe if histories are sanitized, access-controlled, retained briefly, and kept out of source control.

faq.question: Does it require Docker?
faq.answer: Docker is used in the local setup examples, but unit tests can use Temporal testing utilities without a full server.

faq.question: Which AI coding tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
